steps.title: Convert the table to f(x) values
steps.content:
The table is for $y=f(x)+4$, but the question asks about the graph of $y=f(x)$. Subtract 4 from each listed $y$-value:
  • $f(21)=-8-4=-12$
  • $f(23)=8-4=4$
  • $f(25)=-8-4=-12$

steps.title: Use Desmos regression to get f(0)
steps.content: Enter the adjusted points in Desmos as $x_1=[21,23,25]$ and $y_1=[-12,4,-12]$. Then use the quadratic regression row $y_1 \sim a x_1^2+b x_1+c$. Desmos gives $a=-4$, $b=184$, and $c=-2112$, so $f(x)=-4x^2+184x-2112$. The y-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is $f(0)$, which is the constant term $c=-2112$.

steps.title: Quick hand check
steps.content: The equal values $f(21)=f(25)=-12$ show symmetry around $x=23$, so the vertex is $(23,4)$. In vertex form, $f(x)=a(x-23)^2+4$. Using $(21,-12)$ gives $-12=4a+4$, so $a=-4$. Then $f(0)=-4(23)^2+4=-2112$. The answer is -2112.
